heading: Contracts and agreements with Indian tribes
label: 81 Contracts and agreements with Indian tribes
notes: References in TextThe Indian Gaming Regulatory Act, referred to in subsec. (f)(2), is , , , which is classified principally to chapter 29 (§ 2701 et seq.) of this title. Amendments2000— amended section generally, substituting present provisions for provisions which required agreements with Indian tribes or Indians to be in writing, to bear the approval of the Secretary, to contain the names of all parties in interest, to state the time and place of making, purpose, and contingencies, and to have a fixed time limit to run, and provisions which declared agreements made in violation of this section to be null and void and which authorized recovery of amounts in excess of approved amounts, with one half of recovered amounts to be paid into the Treasury.Pub. L. 106–1791958—Par. Second. struck out requirement that contracts with Indian tribes be executed before a judge of a court of record.Pub. L. 85–770Par. Sixth. struck out par. Sixth enumerating contractual elements to be certified to by the judge.Pub. L. 85–770

children.text: The term “Indian lands” means lands the title to which is held by the United States in trust for an Indian tribe or lands the title to which is held by an Indian tribe subject to a restriction by the United States against alienation.
The term “Indian tribe” has the meaning given that term in .section 5304(e) of this title
The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: Approval
children.label: b Approval
children.text: No agreement or contract with an Indian tribe that encumbers Indian lands for a period of 7 or more years shall be valid unless that agreement or contract bears the approval of the Secretary of the Interior or a designee of the Secretary.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: c Exception
children.text: Subsection (b) shall not apply to any agreement or contract that the Secretary (or a designee of the Secretary) determines is not covered under that subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: provides for remedies in the case of a breach of the agreement or contract;

children.label: B
children.text: references a tribal code, ordinance, or ruling of a court of competent jurisdiction that discloses the right of the Indian tribe to assert sovereign immunity as a defense in an action brought against the Indian tribe; or

children.label: C
children.text: includes an express waiver of the right of the Indian tribe to assert sovereign immunity as a defense in an action brought against the Indian tribe (including a waiver that limits the nature of relief that may be provided or the jurisdiction of a court with respect to such an action).

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: e Regulations
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall issue regulations for identifying types of agreements or contracts that are not covered under subsection (b).March 14, 2000
require the Secretary to approve a contract for legal services by an attorney;
amend or repeal the authority of the National Indian Gaming Commission under the Indian Gaming Regulatory Act ( et seq.); or25 U.S.C. 2701
alter or amend any ordinance, resolution, or charter of an Indian tribe that requires approval by the Secretary of any action by that Indian tribe.
